content.full_text:
ANTIOCH CA
CITY HALL • P.O. 130 • ANTIOCH, CA 94509-0504 • (510) 778-3502 • FAX (510) 778-6249

OFFICE OF
THE CITY ATTORNEY

June 10, 1992

Legal Division
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0307

Re: Request for Informal Assistance

Greetings:

Cathryn Freitas, a member of the Antioch City
Council, has authorized and requested me to seek the FPPC's
opinion on the matters stated in this letter.

Mrs. Freitas' spouse is an employee of the Contra
Costa County public works department. He has been designated
as the Program Manager of a joint areawide municipal National
Pollutant Discharge Elimination System ("NPDES") program.

The NPDES is a federally-mandated program that is
enforced through the regional water quality control boards.
Local governmental agencies are required to submit
applications to the regional board, and obtain NPDES permits.
Generally, the function of the NPDES program is to identify
sources of pollutants which may find their way into local
storm drain systems and, ultimately, to protect waters, in
this instance the San Francisco Bay and its delta system. The
goal is to reduce pollutants through elimination of their
discharge into the storm drain system, to educate the public,
and to take other steps such as increased street sweeping.

Contra Costa County, the 18 cities within the
county, and the Contra Costa County Flood Control District
have formed a joint areawide NPDES agency. This agency will
conduct the studies, apply for the permits, and carry out some
or all of the programs necessary. The members will pay, on a
pro rata basis, to Contra Costa County for the costs
associated with this program. Funds for these payments will
come from the members' general operating funds, or they could
be raised by a variety of methods, including the imposition of
special assessments, fees, or charges.

Legal Division
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
June 10, 1992
Page two


     Mr. Freitas' sole duties with the County public
works department are to serve as the program manager of the
joint NPDES program.  The costs to be paid by the members to
the County include reimbursement of Mr. Freitas' salary and
benefits, as well as other administrative overhead and
expenses.  The total of these administrative costs is
$189,412.  Of this sum, $168,000.00 will be paid by the Contra
Costa County Flood Control District, an agency of the county.
Its contribution is specifically designated to reimburse
salary and administrative expenses of the Program Manager (Mr.
Freitas) and his administrative support.  The remaining sum of
$22,000.00 will be paid by the other members, including the
City of Antioch.

     The total cost of the next phase of the project is
$1,084,000.  The City of Antioch's share of this cost is
7.72%, or $70,715.

     It is anticipated that Mr. Freitas' entire salary
and benefits will be reimbursed by the Flood Control
District's contribution of $168,000.00, however, there remains
$22,000.00 of other administrative expenses which will be
borne by the other members, of which Antioch's share would be
$1,698.40.

     Mrs. Freitas inquires whether, under the facts
recited herein, she has a conflict of interest in:

     a)     voting to approve the City's participation in
the program; and

     b)     voting to fund the City's financial
participation by approving the levy of assessments or fees.

     Your response will be very much appreciated.


                                   Very truly yours,

                                   [illegible]
                                   WILLIAM R. GALSTAN
                                   City Attorney


WRG/sh
cc:  Council Member Cathryn Freitas
